right_target.finding: OpenTargets returns 270 evidence rows linking ATM to melanoma (MONDO_0005105), including cancer_gene_census somatic_mutation evidence in nodular and superficial spreading melanoma subtypes plus multiple europepmc literature hits (e.g. PMID 36555667, 34262154, 26275218). GWAS returns 12 SNPs at the ATM locus (11q22.3, e.g. rs4255510), but these are generic intron/intergenic variants with no melanoma trait annotation. Genetic/somatic association is real but ATM is a broad DNA-damage-response gene, not a melanoma-selective driver.
right_target.provenance: OpenTargets_get_evidence_by_datasource (270 rows, cancer_gene_census + europepmc); gwas_get_snps_for_gene (12 SNPs at 11q22.3)

right_tissue.finding: CellMarker returns 6 human records where ATM is a cell marker, all in normal-cell/immune contexts (naive CD8+ T cell, multilymphoid progenitor, microglia, NKT, lung SLC16A7+ cell) plus one gastric cancer central-memory T cell. No melanocyte or melanoma-tissue marker record. ATM expression is broad/ubiquitous rather than melanoma-tissue-selective.
right_tissue.provenance: CellMarker_search_by_gene (search_by_gene, ATM, Human): 6 records, 5 Normal cell / 1 Cancer cell, none melanoma

right_safety.finding: CellMarker shows ATM expressed across normal immune and CNS cell types (T cells, multilymphoid progenitors, microglia, NKT cells), implying broad on-target liability; germline ATM loss causes ataxia telangiectasia (autosomal recessive), confirming systemic essentiality. No ATM-targeted drug exists, so OpenFDA_search_drug_events was not run (no drug to query).
right_safety.provenance: CellMarker normal-cell records (5 normal tissues); Pharos description (ataxia telangiectasia); OpenFDA not applicable (no approved ATM drug)

right_patient.finding: No trial of an ATM-directed intervention in melanoma exists. search_clinical_trials(intervention=ATM, condition=melanoma) returns 1 study, NCT03207347 (niraparib, a PARP inhibitor), which enrolls DDR-deficient tumors including uveal melanoma but targets PARP, not ATM directly; ATM is a stratification/DDR-pathway biomarker, not the drug target. No ATM-drug-in-clinic for melanoma.
right_patient.provenance: search_clinical_trials(intervention=ATM,condition=melanoma): 1 study NCT03207347 (niraparib/PARP, PHASE2, COMPLETED), no direct ATM intervention

right_commercial.finding: No antibody therapeutic against ATM (Thera-SAbDab count=0) and no ATM-targeted clinical program in melanoma. The only related asset is a completed Phase 2 PARP-inhibitor trial. Commercial whitespace with no ATM-directed competitor and no clear ATM melanoma product.
right_commercial.provenance: TheraSAbDab_search_by_target(ATM): 0 therapeutics; search_clinical_trials: only PARP (niraparib) trial, no ATM asset

tractability.finding: Pharos classifies ATM as Tchem (has small-molecule ligands / chemical probes), family Kinase (PI3/PI4-kinase-related serine-protein kinase, UniProt Q13315), with 1363 publications and very low novelty (0.00019), indicating a well-studied, chemically tractable kinase. ATM inhibitors are an established chemotype, so target is druggable in principle despite no melanoma-specific asset.
tractability.provenance: Pharos_get_target(ATM): tdl=Tchem, fam=Kinase, uniprot=Q13315, publicationCount=1363, novelty=0.00019
